title: [CSV reject_errors] csv_line of faulty csv line seems to get truncated between 9995/10000 characters
description:
### What happens?

Importing a long faulty csv line with `ignore_errors=true, store_rejects=true` option results in truncated line being saved into `csv_line` field of reject_errors table

### To Reproduce

Download the attached `bad_policy.csv`.

```
$ duckdb
DuckDB v1.3.2 (Ossivalis) 0b83e5d2f6
Enter ".help" for usage hints.
Connected to a transient in-memory database.
Use ".open FILENAME" to reopen on a persistent database.
```
```sql
create table baduri ("policy" varchar, "data" varchar);
INSERT INTO baduri BY NAME SELECT * FROM read_csv('bad_policy.csv', delim=' ', null_padding=true, nullstr=['-', ''], columns={"policy": varchar, "data": varchar}, escape='"', quote='"', header=true, new_line='\r\n', auto_detect=false, ignore_errors=true, store_rejects=true);
select len(csv_line) from reject_errors;
```
```text
┌───────────────┐
│ len(csv_line) │
│     int64     │
├───────────────┤
│     9999      │
```

[bad_policy.csv](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22308609/bad_policy.csv)

This example shows using cli but its also true with `go-duckdb`

### OS:

Mac OS X

### DuckDB Version:

DuckDB v1.3.2 (Ossivalis) 0b83e5d2f6

### DuckDB Client:

cli

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Ritesh Nadhani

### Affiliation:

Netskope Inc

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Sample', 123.45);
CREATE TABLE reject_errors (csv_line VARCHAR(10000), error_message VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO reject_errors (csv_line, error_message) SELECT 'This is a long faulty csv line that exceeds the limit of characters allowed in the csv_line field, which should trigger an error.', 'Line too long' WHERE LENGTH('This is a long faulty csv line that exceeds the limit of characters allowed in the csv_line field, which should trigger an error.') > 9999;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reject_errors WHERE LENGTH(csv_line) > 9999;
key_bug_features: Truncated csv_line when importing long faulty CSV with options
